name: Somerville Community Corporation
description_short: Somerville Community Corporation is a non-profit offering free financial counseling, job training, and affordable housing programs to low-income residents in Massachusetts.
description_long:
Somerville Community Corporation (SCC) is a Massachusetts-based non-profit organization focused on economic empowerment and housing stability in Somerville and surrounding areas. Founded to address displacement and economic inequality in a rapidly gentrifying city, SCC operates as a community development corporation (CDC) with deep ties to local residents and policy advocacy.

SCC offers three primary service tracks: (1) Real Estate Development, where they develop and preserve affordable rental units, ownership opportunities, and commercial space; (2) Economic Opportunity programs including job placement, financial stabilization counseling, and homeownership readiness training; and (3) Community Building initiatives centered on affordable housing advocacy and resident organizing. All core services appear to be offered at no cost to eligible low-income residents, consistent with their non-profit model and grant funding (including recent $75,000 LISC grant and state affordable housing grants).

SCC distinguishes itself through integrated, multi-service approach connecting housing stability with economic mobility rather than treating them separately. They actively organize residents around policy change and combine direct services (job placement, financial counseling) with systemic advocacy ("development without displacement"). Their work specifically targets communities undergoing rapid gentrification, addressing root causes rather than just symptoms of economic instability.

The primary limitation is geographic scope—services appear concentrated in Somerville, Massachusetts, with limited evidence of remote or online service delivery. The website provides limited detail on specific program eligibility criteria, waitlists, or service capacity. As a CDC, their housing development timeline is measured in years, not months, so housing assistance is not immediate. Financial counseling depth and counselor certification status are not specified on the website.
pros: Completely free services—no fees for job placement, financial counseling, or homeownership programs
HUD-connected affordable housing development with real preservation of rental and ownership units
Integrated approach combining job training, financial counseling, and housing assistance in one organization
Multi-language support (Haitian Creole, Portuguese, Spanish) serving immigrant and refugee communities
Active policy advocacy and resident organizing, not just individual service provision
Recent grant awards demonstrate funder confidence ($75K LISC grant, state affordable housing funding)
Focus on income-building (job creation, career advancement) rather than just budget management
cons: Services limited to Somerville, MA area with no indication of remote counseling or broader geographic reach
Website does not specify counselor certifications (NFCC, HUD-approved status not mentioned)
No details on program eligibility, application process, wait times, or service capacity limits
Housing development cycle is multi-year, so housing assistance is not suitable for immediate needs
Limited information on financial counseling scope—unclear if debt management, credit repair, or budgeting are covered
best_for: Low-income Somerville residents facing displacement due to rising rents
Unemployed or underemployed workers seeking job placement and career advancement in local economy
Immigrants and refugees (particularly Haitian, Portuguese-speaking, Spanish-speaking) needing culturally-competent economic support
First-time homebuyers from low-income backgrounds wanting affordable ownership pathways
diagnosis: Somerville Community Corporation is best for low-income Somerville residents (especially immigrants and people of color) facing displacement or economic instability who want free, integrated support combining job placement, financial counseling, and long-term housing solutions. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Somerville, MA and multi-year timelines for housing assistance—this is not for immediate emergency financial needs or those outside the service area.
services: Affordable rental housing development and preservation
Affordable homeownership programs and down payment assistance
Job placement and local employment matching
Community organizing around affordable housing policy
Commercial space development for local businesses
First Source employment program (job quality initiatives)
Multilingual support in Haitian Creole, Portuguese, and Spanish
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 337 Somerville Ave, Second Floor, Somerville, MA 02143
